title: Microstylis unifolia
intro: Microstylis unifolia, also known as Indian cobra lily or orchid root, is a plant species belonging to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Microstylis unifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Microstylis unifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This species is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. Within the Orchidaceae family, Microstylis unifolia is typically placed in the subfamily Orchidoideae. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a part of the tribe Cranichideae. Further classification may involve the genus Microstylis, which is a part of the orchid subfamily Orchidoideae.
uses: Microstylis unifolia has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and roots are often used in herbal remedies to treat various health conditions.
